# CWE-645: Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism (mitigated, pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-645-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"database/sql"
	"net/http"
	"sync"
	"time"
)

var lockoutMu sync.Mutex
var failedAttempts = map[string]int{}
var lockedUntil = map[string]time.Time{}

// Safe: allows several failures and applies a temporary lockout window.
func Login(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) {
	user := r.FormValue("username")
	pass := r.FormValue("password")
	lockoutMu.Lock()
	if until, ok := lockedUntil[user]; ok && time.Now().Before(until) {
		lockoutMu.Unlock()
		http.Error(w, "forbidden", http.StatusForbidden)
		return
	}
	lockoutMu.Unlock()
	var stored string
	err := db.QueryRow("SELECT password FROM accounts WHERE username = $1", user).Scan(&stored)
	if err != nil || stored != pass {
		lockoutMu.Lock()
		failedAttempts[user]++
		if failedAttempts[user] >= 5 {
			lockedUntil[user] = time.Now().Add(15 * time.Minute)
			failedAttempts[user] = 0
		}
		lockoutMu.Unlock()
		http.Error(w, "unauthorized", http.StatusUnauthorized)
		return
	}
	lockoutMu.Lock()
	failedAttempts[user] = 0
	lockoutMu.Unlock()
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
}
